title: NACP AUTH_ACTION envelope
description: A signed envelope binding a caller to a specific (action, target) pair. Used to authenticate state-changing REST calls (heartbeat, deregister, task creation, ledger writes, etc.) where the request body cannot itself carry the auth signature (GET/DELETE/PUT). The envelope is base64-encoded and sent as ``X-NACP-Auth: <base64>``. New in NACP 1.1.1.

msg_id.description: Fresh UUIDv4 per call. Used by the receiver for replay protection across the time-skew window.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC. Receivers reject envelopes outside the configured skew window (default 300s).

to.description: Recipient identity. For hub-bound calls this is typically the implementation's reserved system id (e.g. ``"neutralaizhub"``).

action.description: Implementation-namespaced action string (e.g. ``hub.heartbeat``, ``hub.task_create``). Implementations MUST advertise their full action set via ``.well-known/nacp.auth.action_registry``.

agent_id.description: Target agent the action operates on. Often equal to ``from`` for self-scoped calls; differs for actions that operate on another agent's resources.

signature.description: Base64-encoded Ed25519 signature over the canonical-JSON form of the envelope (with the ``signature`` field stripped).
